canonical_name: Mírzá Muḥammad-Báqir (builder of the Bábíyyih; lieutenant of Quddús)
db_statements: Mullá Ḥusayn gave Mírzá Muḥammad-Báqir, who had built the Bábíyyih, the preliminary instructions regarding the design of the fort which was to be constructed for their defence.
‘Leave me alone with him,’ were the words of Quddús as he bade Mírzá Muḥammad-Báqir close the door

canonical_name: Qulí (companion of Ḵhurásán, wounded at Ṭabarsí)
db_statements: No one except a man named Qulí, who rode in advance of Quddús, was badly wounded.

Siyyid Mírzá Ḥusayn-i-Mutavallí, accompanied by his servant, left the fort and went directly to join the prince in his camp.
